refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5 days). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via incident log and safety manager sign-off.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, productive task execution from remote location.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety policies, alerting operators and blocking unsafe actions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (task count, volume, time, incidents) for KPI evaluation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with real-time teleoperation and safety interlock, supported by continuous telemetry collection and KPI reporting.
DP-B: Deploy a modular vehicle platform with plug-and-play teleoperation and safety modules, enabling rapid configuration and upgrade for different productivity targets.
DP-C: Implement a hybrid control architecture (local + remote) with adaptive task planning, safety override, and continuous performance monitoring to maximize productivity within safety constraints.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity autonomous/semi-autonomous vehicle with real-time teleoperation and safety interlock, supported by continuous telemetry collection and KPI reporting.

potential_sources: Vehicle control logic and teleoperation command latency may couple if network link is unreliable; mitigated by safety interlock and local autonomy fallback.
Safety services and vehicle actuation are tightly coupled by design (safety override must block unsafe commands); this is intentional and acceptable.
Telemetry collection and productivity reporting are decoupled from real-time control; acceptable latency for KPI reporting.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits acceptable coupling: safety-critical coupling is intentional and managed via interlock; non-critical couplings (telemetry, reporting) are asynchronous. No evidence of unintended circular dependencies or hidden feedback loops.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity target is achievable with current vehicle platform and operator skill level; risk: target may be unrealistic, requiring vehicle redesign or operator training.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency (network + processing) is ≤ acceptable threshold for safe operation; risk: network congestion or hardware bottleneck may violate latency SLA.
Assumption: Safety interlock logic is correctly specified and implemented; risk: logic errors or sensor failures may allow unsafe states.
Assumption: Worksite safety plan is well-defined and enforceable via automated alerts; risk: ambiguous or conflicting policies may cause false positives or operator override.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) vehicle autonomy level (SAE L0–L5 equivalent), (2) teleoperation latency budget (e.g., ≤500 ms end-to-end), (3) safety interlock decision logic (e.g., fail-safe defaults), (4) telemetry sampling rate and KPI calculation method. This will reduce ambiguity and enable clearer IM assignment.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Responsible for end-to-end productivity KPI achievement, telemetry aggregation, and compliance reporting. Owns the integration logic, interfaces, and acceptance criteria.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Productivity metrics (task count, volume, time, cycle time, incidents) streamed to external KPI reporting system or customer dashboard. Frequency: 1 Hz or event-driven.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Task definitions (dig location, load target, dump site) received from site manager or autonomous task planner. Format: structured task queue with priority and constraints.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Safety alerts and warnings (e.g., proximity hazard, policy violation, interlock activation) sent to Safety Manager and Operator. Frequency: event-driven, latency ≤100 ms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication channel for teleoperation commands, telemetry, and safety signals. Supports both on-site and remote operator connectivity.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. All sub-functions (vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, telemetry) are logically contained within this module. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary. Ownership is clear: System Engineer or Project Manager.

Vehicle module contains the physical earthmoving capability and local autonomy, but does not own teleoperation, safety services, or KPI reporting. Assigning the entire FR to Vehicle would require Vehicle to own interfaces (Teleoperation, SafetyAlert, Telemetry) that logically belong to higher-level integration. High diffusion risk: Vehicle would need to coordinate with external teleoperation and safety modules, violating cohesion.

Teleoperation module provides operator control and situational awareness, but does not own vehicle actuation, safety interlock, or productivity measurement. Assigning the entire FR to Teleoperation would require it to own Vehicle and Safety Services, which are separate modules. High diffusion: Teleoperation is a component of the solution, not the complete solution.

Safety Services module enforces safety policies and alerts, but does not own vehicle control, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning the entire FR to Safety Services would require it to own Vehicle and Teleoperation, which are separate modules. Very high diffusion: Safety Services is a critical component but not the primary driver of productivity KPI.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (or Project Manager as system integrator)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the correct IM boundary because it logically contains all sub-functions required to achieve the productivity KPI: vehicle control, teleoperation, safety services, and telemetry. The module owns the integration logic, interfaces, and acceptance criteria. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and coordinate via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution module may be too coarse-grained, obscuring internal structure and ownership boundaries. Mitigation: Decompose NGHE Solution into Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems at the next level.

Risk: Telemetry collection and KPI calculation may be distributed across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, leading to diffusion and inconsistency. Mitigation: Assign a dedicated Telemetry subsystem (or integrate into a central data aggregation layer) to own KPI calculation and reporting.

Risk: Safety interlock logic may be split between Safety Services and Vehicle modules, creating coupling and verification complexity. Mitigation: Clearly define the safety interlock interface (e.g., SafetyAlert and command veto) and assign ownership to Safety Services with Vehicle as the executor.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution is the correct top-level IM boundary and that Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services are sub-modules within it. Define the internal interfaces between these sub-modules (e.g., Vehicle Control, Safety Override, Telemetry Feed) to clarify ownership and reduce diffusion. Assign a dedicated owner (System Engineer or Integration Lead) to manage the NGHE Solution module and coordinate sub-module interactions.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR clearly states the productivity outcome (≥ productivity_target m³/h or ton/h) and the constraints (safety compliance, operational policies). DP-A (integrated vehicle + teleoperation + safety interlock + telemetry) logically addresses all aspects of the FR: vehicle executes tasks, teleoperation enables operator control, safety services enforce policies, and telemetry measures productivity. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Risk: DP-A specifies 'real-time teleoperation' but does not define latency budget or network reliability requirements. IM assignment to NGHE Solution assumes these are managed at the integration level; recommend explicit SLA definition.

Risk: DP-A specifies 'safety interlock' but does not define the decision logic or failure modes. IM assignment assumes Safety Services module owns the logic; recommend detailed safety specification at next decomposition level.

Risk: DP-A specifies 'continuous telemetry collection' but does not define sampling rate, data format, or storage. IM assignment assumes a dedicated telemetry subsystem; recommend architecture decision at next level.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module contains all sub-functions required by DP-A. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are clearly defined and do not diffuse into sub-modules. Ownership is clear: System Engineer or Integration Lead manages NGHE Solution and coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services subsystems.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the refined FR is feasible via representative operations trial: measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period, log safety incidents, and verify compliance with worksite safety plan. Acceptance criteria are quantitative and measurable. Telemetry interface (Telemetry) enables automated KPI reporting and reduces manual measurement burden.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is allocated to NGHE Solution module, which owns the integration logic and acceptance criteria. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A to specify latency budget (e.g., ≤500 ms end-to-end for teleoperation), safety interlock decision logic (e.g., fail-safe defaults), and telemetry sampling rate (e.g., 1 Hz for KPI metrics).

Define the internal interfaces between Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems (e.g., Vehicle Control, Safety Override, Telemetry Feed) to clarify ownership and reduce diffusion.
Assign a dedicated owner (System Engineer or Integration Lead) to manage the NGHE Solution module and coordinate sub-module interactions.
Develop a detailed safety specification for the safety interlock logic, including decision rules, failure modes, and testing strategy.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined and the selected DP is logically sound. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the correct top-level boundary with clear external interfaces. However, the DP and IM are still coarse-grained and require decomposition to clarify internal structure, ownership boundaries, and interface definitions. Specifically, the sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) must be decomposed into the Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems at the next level. This will enable detailed design, implementation planning, and verification strategy.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, productive task execution from remote location.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall monitor and enforce worksite safety policies, alerting operators and blocking unsafe actions.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (task count, volume, time, incidents) for KPI evaluation.

assumptions: Productivity target is achievable with current vehicle platform and operator skill level.
Teleoperation latency (network + processing) is ≤ acceptable threshold for safe operation (assumed ≤500 ms).
Safety interlock logic is correctly specified and implemented with fail-safe defaults.
Worksite safety plan is well-defined and enforceable via automated alerts.
Telemetry collection and KPI calculation are centralized in a dedicated subsystem or data aggregation layer.
Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry subsystems are logically contained within NGHE Solution and coordinate via well-defined interfaces.
open_risks: Risk: Productivity target may be unrealistic, requiring vehicle redesign or operator training. Mitigation: Conduct feasibility study and pilot trial before full deployment.
Risk: Network congestion or hardware bottleneck may violate teleoperation latency SLA. Mitigation: Define latency budget, conduct network simulation, and implement QoS mechanisms.
Risk: Safety interlock logic errors or sensor failures may allow unsafe states. Mitigation: Develop detailed safety specification, conduct FMEA, and implement redundant safety checks.

Risk: Ambiguous or conflicting worksite safety policies may cause false positives or operator override. Mitigation: Clarify safety policies with Safety Manager, implement policy versioning, and conduct operator training.
Risk: Telemetry data loss or corruption may invalidate KPI measurements. Mitigation: Implement data validation, redundant logging, and audit trails.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of productivity target (m³/h or ton/h) and evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5 days).
Worksite safety plan and operational policies (e.g., proximity limits, speed limits, operator qualifications).
Vehicle specifications (capacity, cycle time, autonomy level) and teleoperation system specifications (latency, bandwidth, control interface).
Safety interlock decision logic and failure modes (e.g., fail-safe defaults, sensor redundancy).
Telemetry data format, sampling rate, and KPI calculation method (e.g., task count, volume, time, incidents).
Representative operations trial plan and acceptance criteria (e.g., productivity ≥ target, zero safety incidents).
